div.head: The Ambassador in Spain (Weddell) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 350. The Department’s 200, April 22, 7 p.m., is receiving careful attention as is its 184, April 10, 6 p.m. On receipt of the latter I took steps to bring its essential contents to the attention of the Embassy staff and all our consular officers in Spain. I have also discussed the general subject with every one of my American colleagues and with various other Ambassadors and Ministers. I have also, in various conversations with Spanish officials and others in a position to affect public opinion here, stressed the attitude and viewpoint laid down by the Department.
I again invite attention to my No. 341, April 21, 7 p.m., since I fear a merely informative visit to the Caudillo would fail of practical result.
